title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: Zebra has pre-handshake buffer capacity reservation based on attacker-claimed body length
description:
### Am I affected

You are affected if:

1. You run `zebrad` up to and including `v4.4.1`.
2. Your node accepts inbound P2P connections (`network.listen_addr` is set, which is the default).

### Summary

The P2P codec's `Codec::decode()` method calls `src.reserve(body_len + HEADER_LEN)` after parsing a 24-byte protocol header, using the attacker-claimed `body_len` field. This reserves up to `MAX_PROTOCOL_MESSAGE_LEN` (~2 MiB) of virtual buffer capacity per connection before any body bytes arrive

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: zebrad has mempool transaction admission denial via single-peer inbound queue saturation
description:
### Am I affected

You are affected if:

1. You run `zebrad` up to and including `v4.4.1`.
2. Your node accepts inbound P2P connections (`network.listen_addr` is set, which is the default).
3. Your node's mempool is active (node is synced near the chain tip).

All default configurations are affected.

### Summary

A single unauthenticated P2P peer can monopolize all 25 inbound mempool download/verification slots (`MAX_INBOUND_CONCURRENCY`) by advertising fake transaction IDs. While the slots are

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a single unauthenticated P2P peer to monopolize all inbound mempool transaction slots by advertising fake transaction IDs. This can lead to a denial of service (DoS) condition, preventing legitimate transactions from being processed and potentially disrupting network operations.
recommended_actions: Upgrade `zebrad` to a version newer than v4.4.1 once a patched release is available.
Restrict inbound P2P connections by modifying the `network.listen_addr` configuration to limit exposure.
Implement rate-limiting or peer verification mechanisms to mitigate the risk of single-peer saturation.

title: zebrad vulnerable to full node denial of service via crafted Sapling receiver in z_listunifiedreceivers
description:
### Am I affected

You are affected if:

1. You run `zebrad` up to and including `v4.4.1`.
2. Your `zebrad.toml` sets `rpc.listen_addr` to a TCP address (RPC server is enabled).
3. An attacker can authenticate to the RPC endpoint. With the default `enable_cookie_auth = true`, this requires the attacker to read the `.cookie` file (typically local access). With `enable_cookie_auth = false`, any network client reaching the RPC port can trigger it.

### Summary

The `z_listunifiedreceivers` RPC hand

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to cause a denial of service (DoS) on the affected `zebrad` full node by exploiting the `z_listunifiedreceivers` RPC handler. This could disrupt blockchain operations and availability for nodes running the vulnerable software, potentially impacting services relying on the node for blockchain data.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest version of `zebrad` that addresses this vulnerability, as soon as a patch is available.
Ensure that RPC endpoints are not exposed to untrusted networks and are protected by strong authentication mechanisms.
Restrict access to the `.cookie` file by implementing proper file permissions and limiting local access to trusted users only.

title: Grackle: Fail-open authorization in the MCP tool layer lets scoped agents perform cross-task and cross-session mutations (IDOR)
description:
## Summary

Authorization for scoped (agent) MCP callers is enforced **inline, per tool**, and is applied inconsistently — several mutating tools silently omit the ancestry/workspace check that their siblings perform. Because the MCP server authenticates all outbound gRPC with the full server API key and the backend gRPC handlers perform **no caller-based authorization**, the MCP tool layer is the *sole* authorization boundary. A malicious or prompt-injected scoped agent can therefore perform cr

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: zebrad vulnerable to getblocks/getheaders locator CPU amplification via uncapped vector length
description:
### Am I affected

You are affected if:

1. You run `zebrad` up to and including `v4.4.1`.
2. Your node accepts inbound P2P connections.

### Summary

The `read_getblocks` and `read_getheaders` codec paths accepted block locator vectors up to approximately 65,535 entries (the generic `TrustedPreallocate` ceiling derived from `MAX_PROTOCOL_MESSAGE_LEN`), rather than the protocol-specification limit of 101 entries (matching zcashd's `MAX_LOCATOR_SZ`). Each entry in the locator vector triggers a pe

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: zebrad has full node denial of service via non-ASCII LongPollId in getblocktemplate
description:
### Am I affected

You are affected if:

1. You run `zebrad` up to and including `v4.4.1`.
2. Your `zebrad.toml` sets `rpc.listen_addr` to a TCP address (RPC server is enabled).
3. An attacker can authenticate to the RPC endpoint. With the default `enable_cookie_auth = true`, this requires the attacker to read the `.cookie` file. With `enable_cookie_auth = false`, any network client reaching the RPC port can trigger it.

### Summary

The `getblocktemplate` RPC handler panics when parsing a `Long

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to cause a denial of service (DoS) on the `zebrad` full node by exploiting the `getblocktemplate` RPC handler with a malformed LongPollId. This could disrupt blockchain operations and availability for affected nodes.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of `zebrad` beyond v4.4.1 as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict access to the RPC endpoint by ensuring proper authentication mechanisms are in place, such as keeping `enable_cookie_auth` enabled and securing the `.cookie` file.
Implement network-level restrictions (e.g., firewalls) to limit access to the RPC port to trusted IP addresses only.

title: Mautic has SQL Injection in API Contact Filtering
description:
### Summary
An SQL injection vulnerability exists in Mautic's API contact filtering mechanism. Due to insufficient recursive sanitization of nested query parameters, an authenticated API user can bypass input filtering and inject arbitrary SQL commands.

### Impact
An authenticated user with API access can exploit this vulnerability to execute arbitrary SQL queries against the underlying database. This allows unauthorized retrieval of sensitive database contents—including user credentials, syste

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in Mautic's API contact filtering mechanism allows authenticated API users to execute arbitrary SQL commands. This can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive database contents, including user credentials and system data, potentially compromising the entire application.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Mautic to the latest patched version addressing CVE-2026-4776, if available.
Restrict API access to trusted users and implement strict access controls to minimize exposure.
Conduct a thorough review of database logs for any signs of suspicious activity or unauthorized access.

title: Froxlor customer can create MySQL databases on disallowed servers via Mysqls.add API
description:
## Summary

The `Mysqls.add` API command (`lib/Froxlor/Api/Commands/Mysqls.php`) accepts a customer-controlled `mysql_server` parameter and only validates that the value is numeric and that the server index exists in `userdata.inc.php`. It never checks the value against the calling customer's `allowed_mysqlserver` allowlist. A customer can therefore create a database, plus a MySQL user with a password they choose, on any MySQL server the operator has configured — including servers that were expl

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: Froxlor: Authenticated customers can read other customers' allowed sender aliases
description:
## Summary

An authenticated customer can read other customers' allowed sender aliases from Froxlor's sender-delete confirmation page when `mail.enable_allow_sender` is enabled. `customer_email.php` loads `allowed_sender` by global auto-increment `senderid` alone, so a customer can enumerate foreign sender alias IDs and make Froxlor disclose those values in the confirmation dialog for the attacker's own mailbox.

## Details

The vulnerable read lives in `customer_email.php`:

```php
$senderid =

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to enumerate and access other customers' allowed sender aliases, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive email configuration data. This could result in privacy violations, phishing attacks, or misuse of email systems.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Enterprises using Froxlor
recommended_actions: Immediately update Froxlor to the latest version if a patch is available to address this vulnerability.
Disable the 'mail.enable_allow_sender' feature temporarily if it is not critical to operations.
Implement additional access control checks to ensure that users can only access their own sender alias data.

title: electerm has Command Injection in File System Operations (rmrf, mv, cp)
description:
### Impact

A command injection vulnerability exists in electerm's file system operations (`rmrf`, `mv`, `cp`) in `src/app/lib/fs.js`. These functions construct shell commands by interpolating file paths directly into command strings without escaping shell metacharacters.

**Vulnerable functions:**
- `rmrf()` - Uses `rm -rf "${path}"` (double quotes, vulnerable to `"` injection)
- `mv()` - Uses `mv '${from}' '${to}'` (single quotes, vulnerable to `'` injection)
- `cp()` - Uses `cp -r "${from}" "

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49255.

title: Dragonfly Manager OAuth provider client_secret disclosure via unauthenticated GET /api/v1/oauth
description:
### Summary

The Dragonfly Manager exposes `GET /api/v1/oauth` and `GET /api/v1/oauth/:id` to unauthenticated clients. The response body deserializes the entire `manager/models.Oauth` struct, which includes the `client_secret` field. Any network-reachable attacker can read the OAuth client secrets configured for `github` or `google` providers, defeating the confidentiality guarantee of those secrets and enabling subsequent abuse against the connected identity providers.

### Affected versions

`

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49254.

title: electerm has Path Traversal in Zmodem and Trzsz Download Filename Handling
description:
### Impact

A path traversal vulnerability exists in the Zmodem and Trzsz file download handlers in electerm. When receiving files via Zmodem or Trzsz protocols, electerm uses the remote-supplied filename directly in `path.join()` with the user-selected download directory without sanitization.

A malicious SSH server or remote shell process can send a specially crafted filename such as `../escaped.txt` to escape the user-selected download directory and write files to arbitrary locations on the u

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The path traversal vulnerability allows attackers to write files to arbitrary locations on the user's system, potentially leading to unauthorized data modification, system compromise, or exploitation of sensitive files.
recommended_actions: Update electerm to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-49253.
Implement strict filename validation and sanitization in file handling processes to prevent path traversal attacks.
Restrict file download permissions and monitor file system activity for unusual behavior.

title: @conform-to/dom parseSubmission vulnerable to CPU exhaustion when parsing many unique form fields
description:
A CPU exhaustion vulnerability exists in Conform's [`parseSubmission`](https://conform.guide/api/react/future/parseSubmission) future API when parsing `FormData` or `URLSearchParams` submissions with many unique field names. The parser previously looked up values by field name, which could require repeated scans of the submitted entries and cause excessive synchronous CPU work if an attacker supplies a crafted submission.

> [!NOTE]
> The patched version fixes this by iterating submitted entries

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49250.

title: Grackle has command/argument injection in the git worktree executor that enables RCE on provisioned hosts via an unsanitized task branch name (shell:true)
description:
## Summary

The default git executor used for all worktree operations spawns `git` through a shell, and the untrusted task **branch** name flows into the command unsanitized. A caller able to reach the PowerLine `SpawnSession` RPC (a malicious or compromised agent acting through the orchestration layer, or any client able to spawn a task) can achieve **arbitrary command execution** as the PowerLine user on every provisioned environment (SSH host, Docker container, or Codespace), escaping the age

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: joserfc: HS256/HS384/HS512 verify accepts empty/nil HMAC key (cross-language sibling of CVE-2026-45363)
description:
### Summary

`joserfc.jwt.decode` accepts attacker-forged HMAC-signed tokens when the
caller-supplied verification key is the empty string or `None`.
`HMACAlgorithm.sign` and `HMACAlgorithm.verify` in
[`src/joserfc/_rfc7518/jws_algs.py:62-70`](https://github.com/authlib/joserfc/blob/1ddca8f3c73ff47e3bc3ac06cb0c08a9535677ec/src/joserfc/_rfc7518/jws_algs.py#L62-L70) feed whatever
`OctKey.get_op_key(...)` produced into `hmac.new(...)`, and `OctKey.import_key`
only emits a `SecurityWarning` when the

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49852.

title: SFTPGo has stored XSS via inline parameter on public shares and user file download
description:
## Summary

The inline query parameter on the browsable-share file download and on the authenticated user file download suppressed Content-Disposition: attachment, so an HTML file stored in a share or home directory could be served as text/html and execute in SFTPGo's web origin (stored XSS).

## Impact

Low. Exploitation requires the attacker to place the file and a victim to open the crafted link — a URL the WebClient never generates, so it requires social engineering — and the practical condi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows stored XSS attacks via crafted HTML files served as text/html in SFTPGo's web origin. Exploitation requires social engineering and user interaction, limiting its practical impact. However, successful exploitation could lead to unauthorized script execution in the victim's browser.
affected_industries: Enterprises using SFTPGo for file sharing
recommended_actions: Update SFTPGo to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-49245.
Implement strict Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to mitigate XSS risks.
Educate users about the risks of clicking on unverified or suspicious links to reduce the likelihood of successful social engineering attacks.

title: SFTPGo has path confinement bypass in public browsable share partial ZIP download
description:
## Summary

The public web-client endpoint for partial ZIP downloads of a browsable share did not correctly confine the client-supplied files entries to the shared directory. A requester able to reach a public share could read files located outside the shared directory, as long as the target's canonical path begins with the shared directory's name.

## Patches

Fixed in v2.7.3. The fix replaces the raw prefix check with a directory-boundary–aware check.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49244.

title: @asymmetric-effort/specifyjs: CSS expression sanitization is bypassable in renderToString
description:
## Finding

**Location**: `core/src/server/render-to-string.ts:307-311`

CSS value sanitization stripped `expression(` and `url(javascript:` using simple regex, but could be bypassed with CSS unicode escapes (`\65xpression(`), null bytes, or CSS comments (`exp/**/ression(`).

**Mitigating Factor**: These CSS injection vectors only work in legacy browsers (IE6-IE10). SpecifyJS targets modern browsers.

## Status

**Fixed in v0.2.136** — CSS sanitization now normalizes unicode escapes and strips C

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows CSS injection by bypassing sanitization mechanisms, potentially enabling attackers to execute malicious code in legacy browsers (IE6-IE10). However, the impact is limited as SpecifyJS primarily targets modern browsers.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to SpecifyJS version 0.2.136 or later to ensure the vulnerability is patched.
Audit your codebase for any dependencies or libraries using older versions of SpecifyJS and update them.
If supporting legacy browsers is necessary, implement additional server-side sanitization to mitigate CSS injection risks.

title: @asymmetric-effort/specifyjs: No redirect target validation in secureFetch
description:
## Finding

**Location**: `core/src/shared/secure-fetch.ts`

`assertSecureUrl` validated only the initial request URL. The `fetch()` API follows redirects by default (up to 20 hops). A request to a valid `https://` URL could redirect to `http://internal-service/` or other unvalidated destinations.

## Status

**Fixed in v0.2.136** — `secureFetch` now defaults to `redirect: 'error'` which rejects any redirect. Callers can override with `{ redirect: 'follow' }` if they trust the target.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit unvalidated redirect targets in the 'secureFetch' function, potentially exposing sensitive internal services or enabling man-in-the-middle attacks. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized access, or service disruptions.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to version 0.2.136 or later of the @asymmetric-effort/specifyjs library to ensure the vulnerability is patched.
Audit all applications using the 'secureFetch' function to identify and mitigate any potential misuse of the 'redirect' option.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect suspicious redirect behavior in applications using this library.

title: @asymmetric-effort/specifyjs: `data:` URI allowed without size restriction
description:
## Finding

**Location**: `core/src/shared/secure-fetch.ts:33-35`

`data:` URIs were allowed without any restriction. While `data:` URIs don't make network requests, they can be used for memory exhaustion via very large data URIs.

## Status

**Fixed in v0.2.136** — `data:` URIs are now limited to 1MB. URIs exceeding this limit throw an error.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: CVE-2026-9786 - Quest NetVault Backup NVBUDashboard SQL Injection Remote Code Execution Vulnerability. This vulnerab
description:
Quest NetVault Backup NVBUDashboard SQL Injection Remote Code Execution Vulnerability. This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations of Quest NetVault Backup. Although authentication is required to exploit this vulnerability, the existing authentication mechanism can be bypassed.

The specific flaw exists within the processing of NVBUDashboard JSON-RPC messages. The issue results from the lack of proper validation of a user-supplied string before using it to construct SQL queries. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to execute code in the context of NETWORK SERVICE. Was ZDI-CAN-27626.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9786.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on Quest NetVault Backup installations, potentially leading to full system compromise, data theft, or service disruption. The authentication bypass increases the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patch or update provided by Quest to address CVE-2026-9785.
Implement network segmentation to restrict access to Quest NetVault Backup systems and limit exposure to potential attackers.
Enable robust monitoring and logging to detect any unauthorized access or suspicious activity related to NVBULibrarySlot JSON-RPC messages.

title: CVE-2026-9780 - Quest NetVault Backup addclient3 Cross-Site Scripting Authentication Bypass Vulnerability. This vuln
description:
Quest NetVault Backup addclient3 Cross-Site Scripting Authentication Bypass Vulnerability. This vulnerability allows remote attackers to bypass authentication on affected installations of Quest NetVault Backup. User interaction is required to exploit this vulnerability in that the target must visit a malicious page or open a malicious file.

The specific flaw exists within the addclient3 webpage. The issue results from the lack of proper validation of user-supplied data, which can lead to the injection of an arbitrary script. An attacker can leverage this in conjunction with other vulnerabilities to execute arbitrary code in the context of SYSTEM. Was ZDI-CAN-27666.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9780.

title: CVE-2026-40079 - Cacti is an open source performance and fault management framework. Versions 1.2.30 and prior are vu
description: Cacti is an open source performance and fault management framework. Versions 1.2.30 and prior are vulnerable to Command Injection due to lack of sanitization in the escape_command() function. The escape_command() function at lib/rrd.php is a no-op: it returns $command unchanged. The command line built by rrdtool_function_graph() is passed through this function and then to shell_exec($full_commandline). The risk is in __rrd_execute() where text_format values from graph templates (which may contain host variable substitutions) reach shell_exec without adequate escaping. This issue has been addressed in version 1.2.31.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40079.

title: CVE-2026-39951 - Cacti is an open source performance and fault management framework. Versions 1.2.30 and prior have a
description: Cacti is an open source performance and fault management framework. Versions 1.2.30 and prior have a Stored SQL Injection vulnerability through graph_name_regexp in the Reports feature. This issue has been fixed in version 1.2.31.
